Vibe Readiness
Review.

A structured security audit of your vibe-coded app. We go through everything an attacker would look for — then tell you exactly what's exposed, what's broken, and what to fix first.

You get a clear verdict: ship or don't ship — plus the prioritized fix list to get there.

Request a Review — $300 See what's included
vibeflare — readiness-scan

$ vibeflare scan --app your-lovable-app

→ Connecting to project...

CRITICAL Supabase service_role key exposed in JS bundle

CRITICAL RLS disabled on users table — all rows readable

WARN    No rate limiting on /api/auth/login

WARN    Stripe key readable from client-side code

WARN    Admin routes lack auth verification

PASS     SQL injection (parameterized queries detected)

PASS     HTTPS enforced on all routes

VERDICT: Do not ship. 2 critical issues require immediate fix.

Full report → readiness-report-2025.pdf

of AI-generated code introduces critical vulnerabilities

1 in 5

vibe-coded apps have serious, exploitable security gaps

Wiz Research, 2025

typical turnaround from access to full written report

VibeFlare SLA

What We Check

The six areas where vibe-coded apps break.

These aren't hypothetical risks. They're the exact vulnerabilities found in thousands of live apps built on Lovable, Replit, Cursor, and Bolt — confirmed by independent security research.

01

Authentication

Can someone access your app without logging in? Can they log in as someone else? AI-built auth is frequently bypassable through client-side tricks or undocumented API routes.

→ Login and signup flow verification

→ Route protection on authenticated pages

→ Session handling and token expiry

→ Password reset flow integrity

02

Data Access Rules

The most common failure in Supabase-backed apps: Row-Level Security exists but doesn't actually restrict anything. User A can read User B's data. We verify your rules enforce what they claim to enforce.

→ Supabase RLS policy correctness (not just existence)

→ Cross-user data access testing

→ Admin vs. user privilege separation

→ Database direct-access exposure

03

Exposed Credentials

API keys, database passwords, and Stripe keys baked into frontend code are readable by anyone who views source. In 400+ confirmed cases, Supabase service keys were found in JavaScript bundles of live apps.

→ Frontend bundle secrets scan

→ Environment variable configuration audit

→ API key scope and permission review

→ Third-party integration credential hygiene

04

API & Endpoint Security

AI-generated APIs frequently skip the basics: no authentication checks, no limits on how many requests someone can make, no validation of what gets sent in. These are the entry points attackers go to first.

→ Unauthenticated endpoint enumeration

→ Abuse protection (login, signup, contact)

→ Input validation and injection risk

→ Admin and internal API exposure

05

Infrastructure Configuration

Default settings on Vercel, Supabase, and Firebase are often not production-safe. We check that your deployment environment isn't leaving doors open by default.

→ Vercel / Netlify config and preview protection

→ HTTPS enforcement and redirect checks

→ Storage bucket and asset access settings

→ Platform security defaults review

06

Third-Party Integrations

Stripe, Resend, OpenAI, Clerk — every integration is a new surface. We check that webhooks are verified, keys are scoped correctly, and integrations can't be abused to trigger unintended charges or data access.

→ Webhook signature verification

→ Payment flow integrity (Stripe)

→ AI API key exposure and cost injection risk

→ OAuth and SSO configuration

What You Get

A report you can act on. Not a spreadsheet of problems.

Security audits are only useful if you know what to do next. Every Readiness Review delivers four things.

1

Ship / Don't Ship Verdict

A clear, unambiguous call on whether your app is safe to go live. No hedging. If there's a critical issue blocking launch, we say so directly.

2

Prioritized Fix List

Every finding is labeled Critical, Warning, or Informational — with plain-language explanations of the risk and specific instructions for fixing it. You know what to do first and why.

3

Written Report

A structured PDF you can share with investors, enterprise customers, or your co-founder. Documents what was checked, what was found, and your security posture at time of audit.

4

30-Minute Debrief Call

We walk through the report together. Ask any question. Most founders leave knowing exactly what they'll fix and in what order.

How It Works

Four steps. Typically done in 48 hours.

We've kept this as simple as possible. You don't need to understand security to go through it.

01

You book and share access

Submit your project details and give us read access to your repo and platform. We'll send a checklist of exactly what we need — nothing more.

02

We run the audit

We manually test and review all six areas within 48 business hours. No automated scan-and-send. A human goes through your app.

03

You receive the report

Written findings land in your inbox. Verdict up front, full detail below. Everything explained in plain language — no security jargon.

04

We debrief together

30-minute call to walk through what we found. Ask anything. Most founders leave knowing exactly what they'll fix and in what order.

This Is Right For You If

You built something people are actually using.

✓

You built with Lovable, Replit, Cursor, Bolt, or Firebase Studio

✓

You have real users — or you're about to go live

✓

Your app collects user accounts, handles payments, or stores any personal data

✓

You don't have a technical co-founder or security background yourself

✓

A breach, data leak, or unexpected bill from misused API keys would be a serious problem for your business

Probably Not Right If

You're still in pure prototype mode.

—

No real users yet and no plans to launch in the next 30 days

—

The app doesn't store user data or take payments — purely read-only or personal use

—

You need enterprise compliance documentation (SOC 2, ISO 27001) — that's a different engagement

Not sure which applies to you? Tell us what you're building and we'll let you know.

Pricing

Flat rate. No surprises.

One audit covers all six areas. All deliverables included.
